info.title: CXOne Expert Core Service
info.description: Welcome to the CXOne Expert API reference documentation! This guide is your starting point to begin leveraging the CXOne Expert API to create advanced content integrations, automate workflows, and download analytics data. The migration of API documentation from the previous guide to this location is an ongoing effort. You are welcome to browse the old API Calls guide, in the event that the API endpoints or use cases you are looking for are not yet migrated. API Calls Reference API methods and call examples to use the CXOne Expert API.

tags.name: Context
tags.description: Every resource on the CXOne Expert site (pages, users, groups, etc.) is available through the API. Authenticated CXOne Expert site users can access the API, through the web browser," once they have signed into the CXOne Expert site. System and application integrations require the use of API Tokens to access the API.;

parameters.name: abort
parameters.description: Specifies condition under which to prevent the save; default is never

parameters.name: redirects
parameters.description: If zero, do not follow page redirects.

parameters.name: tidy
parameters.description: Determines if invalid content is converted to text or removed (default: 'convert')

parameters.name: reltopath
parameters.description: Page used for path normalization. Ignored if relto parameter is defined. (default: none)

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: New page revision is created when no changes are detected when overwrite is true (default: false)

parameters.name: restriction
parameters.description: Optionally set the restriction of the page

parameters.name: ordered
parameters.description: If this is a new hierarchy make it ordered, and put this page at the front

parameters.description: Specifies conditions under which to allow the update when an error would normally be thrown, comma separated for multiple values; default is none.

response.value:
<kernels>
    <kernel id="ced21508-8feb-a714-8197-ae903a51f8f3">
        <score>0.800943970680237</score>
        <chunk>Possible Solutions Why is it important to ensure the Lid seals correctly?
            A freezer that is not sealing properly may lead to the following: Frost build-up Temperature fluctuation
            Lid switch is unable to make contact correctly Are food packages or beverage bottles blocking the Lid?
            Rearrange containers so that they fit more tightly and take up less space. Does the lid gasket seem dirty or sticky?
            The gasket needs to be cleaned regularly in order for the Lid to open and close properly. Dirt and other particles can build up on the gasket
            surface and can cause resistance. Check the lid gasket regularly to ensure it is clean and free of food particles.  CLICK HERE for information on
            how to clean the lid gasket. Is the freezer level? It is important to ensure the freezer is level before aligning the doors.
            A properly leveled freezer will help with the overall proper operation of the freezer.
            Please refer to your Owner's Manual or Installation Instructions for specific leveling information for your freezer.
            Tips for leveling the freezer:  Use a level to make sure the freezer is level from side to side and front to back. Check to make sure the floor
            under the freezer is also level, if the floor is not level shims may need to be used.</chunk>
        <page id="1479" guid="310cbbd510764eafbebf596cda9480d2" draft.state="inactive" href="https://testing.mindtouch.us/@api/deki/pages/1479?redirects=0" deleted="false">
            <uri.ui>https://testing.mindtouch.us/Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Freezer_Lid_Not_Sealing_or_Hard_to_Close_-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</uri.ui>
            <title>Freezer Lid Not Sealing or Hard to Close - Convertible Chest Freezer</title>
            <path seo="true">Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Freezer_Lid_Not_Sealing_or_Hard_to_Close_-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</path>
            <namespace>main</namespace>
            <date.created>2023-09-12T17:05:36Z</date.created>
            <language>en-US</language>
        </page>
    </kernel>
    <kernel id="44cbb6ae-3df2-d744-140c-925103bb96cd">
        <score>0.787060260772705</score>
        <chunk>Possible Solutions Is the lock mechanism preventing the lid from closing?
            (Style of lock may vary by model number) To lock or unlock the freezer, you must hold the key in the lock while turning.
            The lock is spring-loaded, so the key cannot be left in the lock. Clear any frost or debris build-up from lock mechanism that may
            be preventing the lid from closing fully. Unlock and open the lid, close and lock again. Is the freezer level? If the freezer is not level
            it could the lock to not latch correctly.  Level the freezer from side to side and front to back. CLICK HERE for more information on how to level your freezer.
            Are the shelves and/or baskets out of position (on some models)? If the baskets and/or shelves are out of the position is could cause the following:
            Not allow the lid to properly close Not allow the lid to properly open Allow moisture and air in, potentially causing excessive frost build-up
            Cause compressor to run longer to maintain temperatures Lock to not to properly engage  Is there anything preventing the lid from closing?
            Check to make sure food packages are not preventing the lock from latching correctly. Rearrange containers so that they fit more tightly and take up less space .
            If your freezer was recently installed, make sure all packaging has been removed. Some packaging used to avoid damage during shipping could keep the lid from
            opening and closing properly.  Remove any material, items, or debris from the Lid area.</chunk>
        <page id="1483" guid="1c47ebe327144e45892972e1ab05baeb" draft.state="inactive" href="https://testing.mindtouch.us/@api/deki/pages/1483?redirects=0" deleted="false">
            <uri.ui>https://testing.mindtouch.us/Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Lock_Not_Working__-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</uri.ui>
            <title>Lock Not Working - Convertible Chest Freezer</title>
            <path seo="true">Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Lock_Not_Working__-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</path>
            <namespace>main</namespace>
            <date.created>2023-09-12T17:05:36Z</date.created>
            <language>en-US</language>
        </page>
    </kernel>
    <kernel id="19bfed57-dbbd-8e9f-5339-8d1c1148952c">
        <score>0.780667126178741</score>
        <chunk>Possible Solutions Does the gasket (seal) seem dirty and/or sticky? The gasket(s) needs to be cleaned for the door to open and close properly.
            Dirt and other particles can build up on the gasket surface and can cause resistance or improper sealing.  Clean the gasket and the surface that it touches.
            Rub a thin coat of paraffin wax on the gasket following cleaning. Is the beverage center level? If the beverage center is not level, it could cause the door
            not to seal properly.  Level the beverage center  from side to side and front to back so that the door will seal properly.  Is there anything preventing the
            door from closing? Check to make sure food packages are not preventing the door from closing properly. Rearrange containers so that they fit more tightly and
            take up less space . If your beverage center was recently installed, make sure all packaging has been removed. Some packaging used to avoid damage during
            shipping could keep the door from opening and closing properly.  Remove any material, items, or debris from the door area.</chunk>
        <page id="1710" guid="f95620e1fedd4651834f906730625c45" draft.state="inactive" href="https://testing.mindtouch.us/@api/deki/pages/1710?redirects=0" deleted="false">
            <uri.ui>https://testing.mindtouch.us/Refrigeration/Undercounter_Refrigeration/Beverage_Center/Door_Concerns/Doors_are_Not_Sealing_Properly_-_Beverage_Center</uri.ui>
            <title>Doors are Not Sealing Properly - Beverage Center</title>
            <path seo="true">Refrigeration/Undercounter_Refrigeration/Beverage_Center/Door_Concerns/Doors_are_Not_Sealing_Properly_-_Beverage_Center</path>
            <namespace>main</namespace>
            <date.created>2023-09-12T17:05:52Z</date.created>
            <language>en-US</language>
        </page>
    </kernel>
</kernels>

head.description: Retrieve file attachment content (Note: image manipulation arguments are ignored for HEAD requests)

parameters.name: {pageid}
parameters.description: Either an integer page id, "home", ":" followed by a page guid, or "=" followed by a double uri-encoded page title

parameters.name: {filename}
parameters.description: Double uri-encoded file name - should be prefixed with "=" if it does not have an extension

parameters.name: {fileid}
parameters.description: Identifies a file by id

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Height of the image

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Width of the image

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Return a resized image from one of the preset cached sizes; use 'thumb' or 'webview' to return a smaller scaled image; use 'bestfit' along with height/width to return one of the known sizes being at least the size given (default: original)

parameters.name: revision
parameters.description: Revision to retrieve (positive integer revision id or revision guid will retrieve the specific revision)

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When fileid is an integer ID, and this parameter is set to true, we'll even return the file info if it was deleted at some point

parameters.name: draft
parameters.description: Fetch the image attached to the draft of this page

parameters.name: redirects
parameters.description: If zero, do not follow page redirects (only applies when {pageid} is present).

parameters.name: nocache
parameters.description: When false, content distribution network is used in public content requests (default: false)

put.description: Replace an existing attachment with a new version or create a new attachment

parameters.name: {pageid}
parameters.description: Either an integer page id, "home", ":" followed by a page guid, or "=" followed by a double uri-encoded page title

parameters.name: {filename}
parameters.description: Double uri-encoded file name - should be prefixed with "=" if it does not have an extension

parameters.name: uploadsource
parameters.description: Who initiated this upload (e.g. mediaManager

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: File attachment description

parameters.name: alttext
parameters.description: File attachment alt text

parameters.name: draft
parameters.description: Fetch the image attached to the draft of this page

parameters.name: redirects
parameters.description: If zero, do not follow page redirects

parameters.name: pageguid
parameters.description: GUID to be assigned to the page should one be needed for this attachment, if none is provided a random one will be issued

parameters.name: allow
parameters.description: Specifies condition under which to allow the update when an error would normally be thrown; default is none

parameters.name: abort
parameters.description: Specifies condition under which to prevent the save; default is never

get.description: Explain how page content is rendered

parameters.name: {pageid}
parameters.description: Either an integer page id, "home", ":" followed by a page guid, or "=" followed by a double uri-encoded page title

parameters.name: redirects
parameters.description: If zero, do not follow page redirects.

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description: Elements to exclude from response document (choice of "inbound", "outbound", "files"; default: exclude nothing)

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Extra elements to include (choice of "contents","prevnext","draft","overview","scripts","related","schedule"; default: include nothing extra)

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When pageid is an integer page ID, and this parameter is set to true, we'll even return the page if it was deleted at some point

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Render content for different uses; default is 'view'

parameters.name: revision
parameters.description: Revision to retrieve (positive integer revision id or revision guid will retrieve the specific revision)

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Contents result format (default: html)

parameters.name: parser
parameters.description: Include specialized contents parsing rules (default: none)

parameters.name: translate
parameters.description: ISO code for translating page contents

parameters.name: renderAsLlmIndexer
parameters.description: If true validate if a section has the attribute data-llm-no-index to do not get content to index

response.value:

parameters.description: Either an integer page id, "home", ":" followed by a page guid, or "=" followed by a double uri-encoded page title

parameters.name: edittime
parameters.description: The previous revision's edit timestamp (yyyyMMddHHmmss) or "now" to bypass concurrent edit check

parameters.description: The edit comment

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The display title (default: use existing title or determine from page path.)

parameters.name: section
parameters.description: The section number.  If zero, append as a new section

parameters.name: xpath
parameters.description: Identifies the portion of the page to update; this parameter is ignored if section is specified

parameters.name: abort
parameters.description: Specifies condition under which to prevent the save; default is never

parameters.name: redirects
parameters.description: If zero, do not follow page redirects.

parameters.name: tidy
parameters.description: Determines if invalid content is converted to text or removed (default: 'convert')

parameters.name: relto
parameters.description: Page used for path normalization (default: none)

parameters.name: reltopath
parameters.description: Page used for path normalization. Ignored if relto parameter is defined. (default: none)

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: New page revision is created when no changes are detected when overwrite is true (default: false)

parameters.name: allow
parameters.description: Specifies conditions under which to allow the update when an error would normally be thrown, comma separated for multiple values; default is none.

<kernels>
    <kernel id="ced21508-8feb-a714-8197-ae903a51f8f3">
        <score>0.800943970680237</score>
        <chunk>Possible Solutions Why is it important to ensure the Lid seals correctly?
            A freezer that is not sealing properly may lead to the following: Frost build-up Temperature fluctuation
            Lid switch is unable to make contact correctly Are food packages or beverage bottles blocking the Lid?
            Rearrange containers so that they fit more tightly and take up less space. Does the lid gasket seem dirty or sticky?
            The gasket needs to be cleaned regularly in order for the Lid to open and close properly. Dirt and other particles can build up on the gasket
            surface and can cause resistance. Check the lid gasket regularly to ensure it is clean and free of food particles.  CLICK HERE for information on
            how to clean the lid gasket. Is the freezer level? It is important to ensure the freezer is level before aligning the doors.
            A properly leveled freezer will help with the overall proper operation of the freezer.
            Please refer to your Owner's Manual or Installation Instructions for specific leveling information for your freezer.
            Tips for leveling the freezer:  Use a level to make sure the freezer is level from side to side and front to back. Check to make sure the floor
            under the freezer is also level, if the floor is not level shims may need to be used.</chunk>
        <page id="1479" guid="310cbbd510764eafbebf596cda9480d2" draft.state="inactive" href="https://testing.mindtouch.us/@api/deki/pages/1479?redirects=0" deleted="false">
            <uri.ui>https://testing.mindtouch.us/Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Freezer_Lid_Not_Sealing_or_Hard_to_Close_-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</uri.ui>
            <title>Freezer Lid Not Sealing or Hard to Close - Convertible Chest Freezer</title>
            <path seo="true">Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Freezer_Lid_Not_Sealing_or_Hard_to_Close_-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</path>
            <namespace>main</namespace>
            <date.created>2023-09-12T17:05:36Z</date.created>
            <language>en-US</language>
        </page>
    </kernel>
    <kernel id="44cbb6ae-3df2-d744-140c-925103bb96cd">
        <score>0.787060260772705</score>
        <chunk>Possible Solutions Is the lock mechanism preventing the lid from closing?
            (Style of lock may vary by model number) To lock or unlock the freezer, you must hold the key in the lock while turning.
            The lock is spring-loaded, so the key cannot be left in the lock. Clear any frost or debris build-up from lock mechanism that may
            be preventing the lid from closing fully. Unlock and open the lid, close and lock again. Is the freezer level? If the freezer is not level
            it could the lock to not latch correctly.  Level the freezer from side to side and front to back. CLICK HERE for more information on how to level your freezer.
            Are the shelves and/or baskets out of position (on some models)? If the baskets and/or shelves are out of the position is could cause the following:
            Not allow the lid to properly close Not allow the lid to properly open Allow moisture and air in, potentially causing excessive frost build-up
            Cause compressor to run longer to maintain temperatures Lock to not to properly engage  Is there anything preventing the lid from closing?
            Check to make sure food packages are not preventing the lock from latching correctly. Rearrange containers so that they fit more tightly and take up less space .
            If your freezer was recently installed, make sure all packaging has been removed. Some packaging used to avoid damage during shipping could keep the lid from
            opening and closing properly.  Remove any material, items, or debris from the Lid area.</chunk>
        <page id="1483" guid="1c47ebe327144e45892972e1ab05baeb" draft.state="inactive" href="https://testing.mindtouch.us/@api/deki/pages/1483?redirects=0" deleted="false">
            <uri.ui>https://testing.mindtouch.us/Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Lock_Not_Working__-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</uri.ui>
            <title>Lock Not Working - Convertible Chest Freezer</title>
            <path seo="true">Refrigeration/Freezers/Convertible_Chest_Freezer/Lid_Concerns_and_Leveling/Lid_Concerns/Lock_Not_Working__-_Convertible_Chest_Freezer</path>
            <namespace>main</namespace>
            <date.created>2023-09-12T17:05:36Z</date.created>
            <language>en-US</language>
        </page>
    </kernel>
    <kernel id="19bfed57-dbbd-8e9f-5339-8d1c1148952c">
        <score>0.780667126178741</score>
        <chunk>Possible Solutions Does the gasket (seal) seem dirty and/or sticky? The gasket(s) needs to be cleaned for the door to open and close properly.
            Dirt and other particles can build up on the gasket surface and can cause resistance or improper sealing.  Clean the gasket and the surface that it touches.
            Rub a thin coat of paraffin wax on the gasket following cleaning. Is the beverage center level? If the beverage center is not level, it could cause the door
            not to seal properly.  Level the beverage center  from side to side and front to back so that the door will seal properly.  Is there anything preventing the
            door from closing? Check to make sure food packages are not preventing the door from closing properly. Rearrange containers so that they fit more tightly and
            take up less space . If your beverage center was recently installed, make sure all packaging has been removed. Some packaging used to avoid damage during
            shipping could keep the door from opening and closing properly.  Remove any material, items, or debris from the door area.</chunk>
        <page id="1710" guid="f95620e1fedd4651834f906730625c45" draft.state="inactive" href="https://testing.mindtouch.us/@api/deki/pages/1710?redirects=0" deleted="false">
            <uri.ui>https://testing.mindtouch.us/Refrigeration/Undercounter_Refrigeration/Beverage_Center/Door_Concerns/Doors_are_Not_Sealing_Properly_-_Beverage_Center</uri.ui>
            <title>Doors are Not Sealing Properly - Beverage Center</title>
            <path seo="true">Refrigeration/Undercounter_Refrigeration/Beverage_Center/Door_Concerns/Doors_are_Not_Sealing_Properly_-_Beverage_Center</path>
            <namespace>main</namespace>
            <date.created>2023-09-12T17:05:52Z</date.created>
            <language>en-US</language>
        </page>
    </kernel>
</kernels>

parameters.description: Return only results for user (allowed: integer user id or 'current' to filter by the current user) (default: results for all users)

parameters.name: lowquality
parameters.description: Find searches that have 0 or more than 3 result

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Start date for result set (format: 'yyyyMMddHHmmss') (default: one month ago)

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: End date for result set (format: 'yyyyMMddHHmmss') (default: now)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to retrieve. Must be a positive number (default: 25)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of items to skip (allowed: a positive number or 0 to not skip any) (default: 0)

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of items to skip (allowed: a positive number or 0 to not skip any) (default: 0)

parameters.name: getcount
parameters.description: Adds total count (default: true)

parameters.description: Time period bucket size (allowed: hour, day, week, month, quarter, year) (default: month)

parameters.name: userfilter
parameters.description: User filter to apply to data (allowed: none, pro, anonymous, community, anonymousorcommunity, communityorpro) (default: none)

parameters.name: queryfilters
parameters.description: Comma separated list of search term filters to apply to data (ex: foo,bar) (default: none)

parameters.name: originfilter
parameters.description: Filter data by an origin (allowed: mt-web, mt-sfdc, mt-zendesk)

parameters.name: sortby
parameters.description: Sort data (allowed: searches, clicks, results, date, position, clickspersearch) (default: searches)

parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description: Sort direction to be used with sortby (allowed: asc, desc) (default: desc)

parameters.name: recommendedfocusfilter
parameters.description: Apply a negative sentiment analysis filter to data (default: false)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return (allowed: 1 - 1000) (default: 100)

parameters.description: Comma separated list of group id filters to apply to data (ex: 100,101,202)
